checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:162-200` and confirmed the recent UI lock-down is already real: when `isInPinnedShotGroup` is true, the clip context menu hides split, delete, mute, and shot-creation actions and only leaves asset-state actions available.

Checked current shot-group menu wiring in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:691-697` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx:78-95`. Today `Delete shot` deletes the member clips and then unpins the group, but step 9 of the plan says delete/deconstruct/unpin should all just remove the group and leave clips in place, which is a user-visible regression against the brief's 'no regression in shot label menu actions' requirement.

Checked the generic edit surfaces in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:220-236` and `:351-386` plus keyboard dispatch in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:68-110`. The plan focuses on drag/resize/sync, but it does not cover single-clip delete, split, or keyboard-triggered track moves for a selected clip inside a pinned group, even though the hard requirement says normal interactions must not be able to break a group except explicit Deconstruct/Delete shot actions.

Checked shot creation via drag-and-drop in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:128-188`. That path creates pinned shot groups directly when a shot is dropped on the timeline, but the plan does not include it even though the new model changes the group shape and invariants from creation time onward.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:332-356` together with `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts:23-45`. Step 2 says to call `projectPinnedShotGroups()` at the start of `assembleTimelineData()`, but `assembleTimelineData()` receives a prebuilt `resolvedConfig` and computes `signature` from that object, while `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry()` constructs `resolvedConfig` before calling `assembleTimelineData()`. If the config is projected inside `assembleTimelineData()` without rebuilding `resolvedConfig`, `rows/meta/config` will diverge from `resolvedConfig`, preview data, and signature state.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:153-395` and found no existing drag/resize guard around `usePinnedGroupSync`; it only uses a debounce timer and then mutates rows/groups. The plan treats an active-interaction guard as something to 'verify at the call site', but in the current repo that guard does not exist and needs explicit design/plumbing rather than a quick check.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx:102-151` and the preview components, and Remotion does in fact render directly from flat `ResolvedTimelineConfig.clips`. The plan is correct that any group-centric editor model still has to materialize flat clips before preview/render.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Searched the broader edit surface and found the same 'individual clip inside a group' concept in `useClipEditing`, `useKeyboardShortcuts`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement`, not just in drag/resize/sync. That means the disconnect problem is broader than the files emphasized in the plan: the repo also has split, delete, mute, and keyboard track-move paths that operate on selected clip IDs and can target a single pinned member.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:244-331` and found `moveSelectedClipsToTrack()` groups selected clips by row, not by pinned-shot-group membership. If one inner clip is selected, ArrowUp/ArrowDown will still plan a per-clip cross-track move, so the broader movement problem is not confined to `useClipDrag.ts` and `multi-drag-utils.ts`.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:128-188` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:98-111`. The broader repository pattern is that pinned groups are created both from selection pinning and from shot drops, so updating only `pinGroup()` is not enough to make first-class groups the sole creation path.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Checked the clone/copy helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts:163-199`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:91-100`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31-39`. All three currently deep-copy only `clipIds` and `imageClipSnapshot`; if `PinnedShotGroup` gains `start` and `children`, those fields will be silently dropped unless every clone path is updated, and the plan does not call out the `migrate.ts` helper even though it runs on ordinary edits via `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry()`.

Checked supporting tests and found dedicated coverage already exists for omitted paths, including `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` for shot-drop-created pinned groups and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` for pinned-group track updates. The validation section lists drag/resize/sync-oriented suites but does not mention these supporting tests, so the planned test surface is still incomplete.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.contexts.ts:98-161` and confirmed the editor context exports clip-level delete/split/move handlers throughout the tool. If the plan changes selection semantics or reroutes mutations through group-level primitives, this context wiring is part of the supporting infrastructure that also needs updating.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked the `pinGroup()` callers in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:411` and `:439`. Both pass `[...selectedClipIds]` where `selectedClipIds` is a `Set`, so the plan is correct that current group creation preserves selection order rather than spatial order.

Checked the callers behind keyboard shortcuts in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:68-110`. Those callers pass the current selection set to `moveSelectedClipsToTrack()` and call `splitSelectedClip()` / `deleteSelectedClip()` for the primary selected clip, which means a pinned group member can still be targeted as an individual clip if selection remains clip-level as proposed in step 10.

Checked config-edit callers in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:337-386` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:99-127`. These callers pass whole `resolvedConfig` objects through `applyEdit({ type: 'config', ... })`, so a solution that relies only on rows-path helpers or on late projection inside `assembleTimelineData()` does not fully handle every caller shape that mutates grouped clips.

flags.concern: Timeline signatures: projecting grouped clip positions inside `assembleTimelineData()` would leave `resolvedConfig`, preview state, and `signature` stale unless the plan also rebuilds them from the projected config.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:332-356` computes rows from `config` but keeps the passed-in `resolvedConfig`; `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts:23-45` builds that `resolvedConfig` before calling `assembleTimelineData()`.

flags.concern: Pinned-group edit surface: Recurring debt: clip-level keyboard and edit actions (`split`, `delete`, ArrowUp/ArrowDown track move) still target selected member clips in `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useClipEditing`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement`, so the plan does not yet contain the full 'only Deconstruct/Delete can break a group' surface.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:68-110`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:220-236`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:351-386`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:244-331` all operate on selected clip IDs, not pinned-group entities.

flags.concern: Shot drop integration: the plan omits `useExternalDrop()` even though shot drops create pinned groups directly, so the new data model is not wired through every creation path.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:128-188` builds `createdClipIds` and appends a new pinned group via `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`.

flags.concern: Pinned-group cloning: adding `start`/`children` without updating every clone path in `migrate.ts`, `timeline-data.ts`, and `usePinnedShotGroups.ts` will silently strip the new fields during ordinary edits.
Shot-group menu semantics: step 9 changes `Delete shot` from delete-plus-unpin to unpin-only, which contradicts current behavior and the brief's no-regression requirement.
flags.evidence: Current wiring in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:694-697` calls `handleDeleteClips(group.clipIds)` and then `unpinGroup(...)`, while the plan says delete/deconstruct/unpin should all just remove the group.
